The Delta IOta Chi sorority girls
spent a delightful afternoon Thurs
day with Miss Susan Mason at her
home on Eighth avenue. A dainty
luncheon was served during the
social hours, and the girls enjoyed
al informal chat.


Mrs. Lulu Loveland Shepard, a
prominent lecturer of . Salt Lake
City, and president of the Woman S
, Christian Temperance Union of
/ Utah, was honor guest at recep-
ton given Thursday afternoon by
the local members Of the w. c. I.
L. at the parlors of the Young
WOman's Christian association.
The rooms were beautifully deco
rated with the national colors of
red, white and blue, lies and car
nations being used with charming
effect. The hours of receiving were
from , until > o'clock.


Many Of The prominent club and
church women OF the city were
present at the occasion.


Mrs. J. NJ. GrimmCr entertained
entertained number Of friends
at pretty Easter luncheon Thurs
day afternoon at her home on
Broadway avenue, the Occas on be-
ing the celebration of her birthday
anniversary ....


The rooms were chalmingly deco
/ rated with smilaX, carnations and
lilies, while the table was effective
! ly trimmed with favors of the
Eastertide, rabbits and Easter eggs
being much in evidence.


The ladies OF the Electa chapter
of the Eastern Star were among the
guests, and they presented the
hostess with beautiful piece of
cut glass.


A color OF pink and white mark
ed the delightful informal party
Thursday evening by Mrs. Robert
Strahorn complimentary to her
sister, Mrs. Hattie +. Lacey.


A court luncheon was served at
1870 O'Clock and was followed by
an afternoon at cards. Covers were
placed for II guests.
